jokes.text: Another Xmas come and gone. And to everyone I didn't kill, season's greetings! And hold still next year. Ho!
jokes.setup: Traditional holiday greeting setup
jokes.punchline: Threatening those he didn't kill to hold still next year for easier murdering
jokes.explanation: Perfect character-driven dark humor. Robot Santa's murderous nature twisted into holiday pleasantries with great economy and threatening follow-up.

jokes.text: We only lost one crew member. Poor Charlie. It was his first day on the job. And he was just a week from retirement.
jokes.setup: Casual mention of losing a crew member
jokes.punchline: Stacking clichéd tragic character tropes (first day + week from retirement)
jokes.explanation: Good structure combining multiple tragedy tropes, though the concept isn't particularly original.

jokes.text: I'm thinking about mint, but I'm tasting pain.
jokes.setup: Contemplating mint flavor
jokes.punchline: Inexplicably tasting pain instead
jokes.explanation: Perfectly absurd Professor line with great economy. The disconnect between expectation and reality is classic Farnsworth.

jokes.text: Mmm, now I'm tasting about thinking.
jokes.setup: Previous line about thinking/tasting confusion
jokes.punchline: Grammatically impossible continuation of the sensory confusion
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation that makes the absurdity even more confusing. 'Tasting about thinking' is wonderfully nonsensical wordplay.

jokes.text: Therefore, you're all on unpaid leave. So, leave.
jokes.setup: Bureaucratic explanation about the post-holiday lull
jokes.punchline: Blunt wordplay on 'leave' as both vacation and departure
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective wordplay delivered in Hermes' characteristic bureaucratic style.

jokes.text: Really? You have something better to do than visit old people in the sewer?
jokes.setup: Fry declining to visit her parents
jokes.punchline: Sarcastically highlighting how unappealing visiting sewer-dwelling parents sounds
jokes.explanation: Solid sarcasm that acknowledges the absurdity of the situation, though not particularly original.

jokes.text: Say hi to Bender's nonexistent parents for me.
jokes.setup: Fry's weak excuse about visiting Bender's parents
jokes.punchline: Calling out the obvious lie since robots don't have parents
jokes.explanation: Good sarcastic call-out that uses established robot lore to expose Fry's lie.

jokes.text: Less than twice, more than nunce.
jokes.setup: Leela asking if he knows how many times 'once' is
jokes.punchline: Creating the nonsense word 'nunce' to complete the mathematical progression
jokes.explanation: Brilliant wordplay creating 'nunce' as the logical predecessor to 'once'. Perfect Fry logic with great economy and quotability.

jokes.text: Eh, must be outta batteries.
jokes.setup: Plant watering device presumably not working
jokes.punchline: Casual assumption that a plant needs batteries
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Fry's confusion about basic biology vs. technology.

jokes.text: I don't know what's going on, but I love circling.
jokes.setup: Fry calls for everyone to circle up
jokes.punchline: Zoidberg's inexplicable enthusiasm for the formation itself
jokes.explanation: Perfect Zoidberg moment - his simple joy in physical formations over understanding context.

jokes.text: Uh, maybe ten? Or 45? Who knows! It's impossible to say with all the time loops we've been through.
jokes.setup: Trying to figure out which anniversary it is
jokes.punchline: Acknowledging the show's complex timeline continuity issues
jokes.explanation: Clever meta-reference to the show's actual continuity complexity, earned through genuine plot history.

jokes.text: That's the beautiful day we met and attempted suicide.
jokes.setup: Fry mentioning their meeting anniversary
jokes.punchline: Bender's romantic framing of their mutual suicide attempt
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark humor calling back to series origins. Bender's romantic phrasing of suicide attempt is perfect character comedy.

jokes.text: How may I pervert you?
jokes.setup: Standard 'how may I help you' greeting
jokes.punchline: Malapropism turning 'help' into sexual offer
jokes.explanation: Simple sexual wordplay, fairly predictable for an adult-oriented shop setting.

jokes.text: 'filthy porno'? Ah, yes. I think I have what you're looking for.
jokes.setup: Fry awkwardly asking for 'filthy porno' as code
jokes.punchline: Shopkeeper taking it literally while it's actually code for fireworks
jokes.explanation: Decent misdirection setup for the fireworks reveal, though the execution is fairly standard.

jokes.text: Right this way for your 'porno,' sir.
jokes.setup: The code word 'porno' for fireworks
jokes.punchline: Clerk playing along with unnecessary air quotes
jokes.explanation: Extends the code word gag with the clerk's obvious wink-wink delivery

jokes.text: Um... I forget what it's called. The language my mouth knows.
jokes.setup: Being asked what language he wants the fireworks to spell in
jokes.punchline: Describing English in the most roundabout way possible
jokes.explanation: Perfect Fry stupidity - unable to remember 'English' but describing it in an oddly poetic way.

jokes.text: Nobody needed Rudy Giuliani anyway.
jokes.setup: Casual disposal of someone from a cryogenic tube
jokes.punchline: Dismissive comment about a real political figure
jokes.explanation: Topical political jab that relies more on audience agreement than comedy craft.

jokes.text: He had faker things to do.
jokes.setup: Explaining Fry's absence from family gathering
jokes.punchline: Calling his excuse fake while using 'faker' as comparative adjective
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay creating 'faker' as a comparative form, showing Leela's irritation.

jokes.text: He couldn't make it. He had faker things to do.
jokes.setup: Parents asking where Fry is
jokes.punchline: Sarcastic dismissal implying his excuse was fake
jokes.explanation: Simple sarcasm showing Leela's irritation with Fry

jokes.text: This one's on mes. I likes to help young loverses.
jokes.setup: Offering help to the young couple
jokes.punchline: Gollum-like speech pattern with pluralized pronouns
jokes.explanation: Obvious Gollum reference for sewer mutant character, reasonably well executed but not original.

jokes.text: Welcome to the world of tomorr-- Intruder! Ow!
jokes.setup: Fry trying to make a grand romantic entrance
jokes.punchline: Getting attacked mid-catchphrase by the person he's surprising
jokes.explanation: Good physical comedy timing with the interrupted catchphrase callback to the show's intro.

jokes.text: I wasn't given a name, but I'm gonna assume it's you.
jokes.setup: Dedicating a song to 'a very special one-eyed lady'
jokes.punchline: Acknowledging the obvious casting choice while breaking fourth wall
jokes.explanation: Decent meta-joke about celebrity cameo limitations, acknowledging the obvious choice.

jokes.text: Thanks. I call it G-water.
jokes.setup: Being offered gin
jokes.punchline: Renaming gin to match his stage name initial
jokes.explanation: Simple celebrity pun playing on Kenny G's name, executed efficiently but not particularly clever.

jokes.text: We don't have a joint bank account. Well, not anymore.
jokes.setup: Fry mentioning spending money from their joint account
jokes.punchline: Revealing he spent it all and it no longer exists
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Fry's financial irresponsibility, well-timed reveal.

jokes.text: We don't have a joint bank account. Well, not anymore.
jokes.setup: Fry mentioning spending from their joint account
jokes.punchline: Revealing he spent so much it's gone
jokes.explanation: Good escalation revealing the extent of Fry's irresponsible spending

jokes.text: Oh snap! She 'Philip'd' you. That is cold.
jokes.setup: Leela calling Fry by his formal name instead of nickname
jokes.punchline: Bender recognizing this as a relationship red flag and naming it
jokes.explanation: Great observation about relationship dynamics, turning formal name usage into a verb.

jokes.text: Hey! You can't do that! That's my only thing!
jokes.setup: Hermes saying 'Good news, everyone!'
jokes.punchline: Professor's territorial reaction to his signature phrase being used
jokes.explanation: Good character moment about the Professor's possessiveness over his catchphrase, self-aware humor.

jokes.text: No, I'm a deep, rich salmon.
jokes.setup: Discussion about red things making the buggalo angry
jokes.punchline: Zoidberg's pretentious correction about his color classification
jokes.explanation: Perfect Zoidberg vanity moment, turning a simple color into a sophisticated descriptor.

jokes.text: genital pics, and so on
jokes.setup: Listing data that dating apps had access to
jokes.punchline: Casual inclusion of intimate photos in the Professor's clinical list
jokes.explanation: Mild shock humor from the Professor's clinical delivery of crude content.

jokes.text: Everything, you whimpering imbecile!
jokes.setup: Fry asking what's wrong with perfect matching
jokes.punchline: Professor's excessive anger at a reasonable question
jokes.explanation: Good Professor outburst, though the insult is fairly standard for his character.

jokes.text: She ran off with the bicycle pump.
jokes.setup: Question about what happened to his previous girlfriend
jokes.punchline: Absurd infidelity scenario with an inanimate object
jokes.explanation: Excellent absurdist humor about robot relationships, perfectly economical and unexpected.

jokes.text: Come back, baby! We can work it out!
jokes.setup: Previous mention of bicycle pump relationship
jokes.punchline: Shopkeeper desperately calling to his bicycle pump lover
jokes.explanation: Good callback extension of the bicycle pump joke, showing it's a pattern not just Bender's experience.

jokes.text: I think it was the new name after the collapse of the USA.
jokes.setup: Characters puzzled by 'USB' acronym
jokes.punchline: Dystopian explanation of USB as post-apocalyptic country name
jokes.explanation: Clever dark humor about American decline through technology confusion.

jokes.text: I have a universal male plug. You never know who you might meet.
jokes.setup: Need for cable connection
jokes.punchline: Zoidberg's sexual interpretation of universal connectivity
jokes.explanation: Good Zoidberg horniness humor with double meaning on 'universal male plug.'

jokes.text: I know Kif and I are soulmates, so this is gonna be fun. Right, Kiffy?
jokes.setup: Confident about soulmate status
jokes.punchline: Kif's uncomfortable silence (implied)
jokes.explanation: Good setup for relationship tension. Character-appropriate confidence from Amy.

jokes.text: It's me! Oh thank the Grand Midwife's porous skin sack. It was always you.
jokes.setup: Mirror reveals Amy as Kif's soulmate
jokes.punchline: Kif's relief with alien-specific exclamation
jokes.explanation: Good character moment with alien-specific dialogue. Shows their relationship dynamic well.

jokes.text: Wow, it just keeps metaphoring.
jokes.setup: Plant dying as relationship metaphor becoming more obvious
jokes.punchline: Turning 'metaphor' into a verb to describe the ongoing symbolism
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta-joke turning 'metaphor' into a verb, acknowledging the heavy-handed symbolism with perfect economy.

jokes.text: Too bad you're not hers.
jokes.setup: Fry celebrating that Leela is his soulmate
jokes.punchline: Hermes crushing reveal that the matching isn't mutual
jokes.explanation: Devastating comedy timing with brutal efficiency. Perfect setup/punchline structure that subverts expectations.

jokes.text: I'll give him a smell assault.
jokes.setup: Need for smelling salts to revive Fry
jokes.punchline: Zoidberg's pun on 'assault' while offering his notorious odor
jokes.explanation: Decent pun that uses Zoidberg's established smelliness, though fairly predictable wordplay.

jokes.text: That's the worst picture of me I've ever seen!
jokes.setup: Seeing Leela's actual soulmate in the mirror
jokes.punchline: Zoidberg thinking the handsome stranger is an unflattering photo of himself
jokes.explanation: Perfect Zoidberg delusion humor, showing his complete disconnect from reality about his appearance.

jokes.text: And they spelled my name wrong!
jokes.setup: Discovering he's not Leela's soulmate
jokes.punchline: Focusing on trivial spelling error instead of devastating romantic news
jokes.explanation: Excellent character comedy showing Fry's misplaced priorities and denial mechanism.

jokes.text: I can't believe it. My little Fry is becoming a murderer.
jokes.setup: Fry heading out to confront Leela's soulmate
jokes.punchline: Bender's proud parental tone about Fry's murder plans
jokes.explanation: Good Bender moment showing his pride in corruption, treating murder like a milestone achievement.

jokes.text: Why didn't it hurt very much?
jokes.setup: Fry attacking Branch with thorns
jokes.punchline: Branch's mild disappointment that the attack wasn't more painful
jokes.explanation: Good character introduction showing Branch's unusual pain tolerance and mild nature.

jokes.text: Of course, you idiot! She's my roommate!
jokes.setup: Branch asking if Fry can introduce him to Leela
jokes.punchline: Fry enthusiastically agreeing to set up his romantic rival
jokes.explanation: Good ironic moment showing Fry's inability to think through consequences of his actions.

jokes.text: I don't like the way that tree is walking.
jokes.setup: Spotting a threatening tree
jokes.punchline: Casual observation about tree locomotion as if it's normal
jokes.explanation: Good deadpan delivery treating impossible tree behavior as merely suspicious rather than impossible.

jokes.text: Hmm, very nice, but how do you know its pronouns?
jokes.setup: Branch explaining the plant is female
jokes.punchline: Fry's modern pronoun awareness about a plant
jokes.explanation: Timely social commentary but feels slightly forced for Fry's character

jokes.text: Really? Well, there's no law against mercy bludgeoning.
jokes.setup: Learning Branch is only injured, not dead
jokes.punchline: Bender's made-up legal concept of 'mercy bludgeoning'
jokes.explanation: Excellent Bender dark humor creating 'mercy bludgeoning' as an oxymoronic legal concept.

jokes.text: You're no son of mine!
jokes.setup: Learning there actually is a law against mercy bludgeoning
jokes.punchline: Bender disowning Fry for being law-abiding
jokes.explanation: Good Bender character moment showing his disappointment in law-abiding behavior.

jokes.text: since you came to this hospital instead of letting me treat you.
jokes.setup: Telling Branch he should recover fine
jokes.punchline: Revealing the good prognosis is because they avoided Zoidberg's medical care
jokes.explanation: Good callback to Zoidberg's incompetent doctoring, self-aware medical malpractice humor.

jokes.text: Yeah, a six-foot-four nobody with really good health insurance.
jokes.setup: Fry dismissing Branch as 'nobody'
jokes.punchline: Branch's practical self-assessment including insurance status
jokes.explanation: Good practical humor acknowledging modern dating realities where health insurance is attractive.

jokes.text: Is it a penis reduction?
jokes.setup: Mention of embarrassing surgery for Branch
jokes.punchline: Zoidberg's inappropriate surgical guess
jokes.explanation: Standard crude humor relying on embarrassing medical procedure, fits Zoidberg but not particularly clever.

jokes.text: Is it a penis reduction? I'm just guessing based on the chart.
jokes.setup: Mentioning Branch's embarrassing surgery
jokes.punchline: Zoidberg's inappropriate medical speculation
jokes.explanation: Zoidberg's inappropriate medical commentary with plausible deniability

jokes.text: Well, I got a really cute skirt on sale, and you've been in a coma for a month. It's plaid.
jokes.setup: Fry asking what happened while he was unconscious
jokes.punchline: Amy prioritizing her shopping success over his month-long coma
jokes.explanation: Perfect Amy character moment showing her priorities, good misdirection structure treating major news as secondary.

jokes.text: He's cheatin' on you, Chastity!
jokes.setup: Reality TV contestant talking about other hot girls
jokes.punchline: Bender getting emotionally invested in reality TV drama
jokes.explanation: Good Bender moment showing his investment in trashy TV, though fairly standard reality TV parody.

jokes.text: Maybe I can convince Branch that Leela and I belong together... if I can just talk to him. Strangle him, you say?
jokes.setup: Fry suggesting reasonable conversation
jokes.punchline: Bender mishearing as violence encouragement
jokes.explanation: Good character interplay - Bender hearing what he wants to hear

jokes.text: Aw, geez! Get a greenhouse.
jokes.setup: Plants having romantic/sexual interaction
jokes.punchline: Fry's 'get a room' equivalent for plants
jokes.explanation: Clever plant-specific version of 'get a room,' good wordplay with perfect economy.

jokes.text: That's it! I'm gettin' my shovel!
jokes.setup: Witnessing Fry's mature, selfless behavior
jokes.punchline: Bender's violent reaction to character growth
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender - can't stand Fry being noble and mature

jokes.text: Yeah, although I have to say he's always plant-splaining stuff to me.
jokes.setup: Discussing Branch's good qualities
jokes.punchline: Plant-specific version of 'mansplaining'
jokes.explanation: Good contemporary social commentary adapted for the plant expert character, effective portmanteau.

jokes.text: I killed it again. Sorry.
jokes.setup: Praising Branch's plant revival skills
jokes.punchline: Immediate admission of destroying it again
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback that shows Fry hasn't learned anything, delivered with casual honesty

jokes.text: sometimes you wanna talk about something other than botany.
jokes.setup: Explaining Branch's flaws
jokes.punchline: Single-minded plant obsession being annoying
jokes.explanation: Realistic relationship complaint about narrow interests
